Motion For Leave To Conduct The Independent Psychological Examination Of Plaintiff Ahmed Alsherif
Off Calendar Jun-04-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Adam Tanouye, Esq. 707-427-3998, McNamara Law Firm for Defendant Yujin Kim,
No other Appearance.

9:44am The court passes the matter at the first calling of the case. No Appearance by Plaintiff.

9:55am The court calls the matter a second time. No appearance by Plaintiff.

Party states appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED.
Court comments, poses questions re counsel of record, admonishes party; The Court prefers to see named counsel on discovery matters instead of new counsel since the matter is about the record, statutory right, respecting process.
Mr. Tanouye responds the Court; lawyers have depos and other matters. Counsel contests and presents arguments; audio recording of the exam, psychologist's moral and ethical obligations conflict with legislature, willing to compromise.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendant's Motion For Leave To Conduct The Independent Psychological Examination Of Plaintiff Ahmed Alsherif is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART.

(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Reported. (302/JMQ)
